channel.title: Frontion - Global Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical headlines from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷🇮🇱 Iran says peace deal requires Israeli withdrawal from Lebanon
item.description: Iran's top diplomat stated that ending the war with the US hinges on Israel withdrawing from southern Lebanon, adding a major complication as Trump pushes to finalize the agreement at the G7 summit. (AP News)

item.title: 🇫🇷🌍 G7 summit opens with Iran deal and Ukraine war dominating agenda
item.description: World leaders gathered in Evian, France for the G7 summit as Trump promotes his preliminary Iran peace deal while allies scramble to keep Ukraine firmly on the agenda alongside Middle East diplomacy. (CNN)

item.title: 🏛️💰 New Fed Chair Warsh faces hawkish shift as first meeting looms
item.description: Kevin Warsh convenes his first Federal Reserve meeting with rate hike expectations rising and the dot plot expected to show a hawkish shift, just weeks after replacing Jerome Powell. (Reuters)

item.title: ⚔️🇱🇧 Israel Lebanon stance threatens to unravel US-Iran agreement
item.description: The US-Iran ceasefire deal hinges on Israel accepting a ceasefire in Lebanon, something it is reluctant to do, creating a key obstacle as mediators try to finalize terms this week. (NPR)

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Japan PM Takaichi pushes coordinated G7 response to China trade tactics
item.description: At the G7 summit, Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi urged allies to develop a united front against Beijing's use of export controls and trade leverage, signaling rising concern over China Shock 2.0. (New York Times)

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky, Trump, Macron Hold Behind-the-Scenes Meeting at G7 Summit
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky met face-to-face with Trump and Macron on the sidelines of the G7 summit in Evian, their first encounter in nearly four months, as Kyiv pushes to revive peace negotiations. (Yahoo News)

item.title: 🇷🇺 Ukrainian Drones Strike Moscow Largest Oil Refinery Miles from Kremlin
item.description: Ukraine launched dozens of drones into Moscow, reportedly causing significant damage to the citys largest oil refinery just miles from the Kremlin, marking the ninth consecutive day of drone attacks on the Russian capital. (MSN)

item.title: 🇦🇺 US Military Plans Permanent War-Ready Weapons Stockpile in Australia
item.description: The US military is planning a permanent war-ready weapons stockpile for its Marine Corps on Australias southeast coast, positioned beyond the range of most Chinese missiles, according to tender documents and officials. (Asharq Al-Awsat)

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Undecided on Sending Military Forces to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Japan said Tuesday it has not yet decided whether its military will join international efforts to help resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz following the US-Iran ceasefire agreement. (Asharq Al-Awsat)

item.title: 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Lukashenko Says Belarus Poses No Military Threat to Ukraine, Apologizes to Zelensky
item.description: Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko stated Belarus poses no military threat to Ukraine and apologized to President Zelensky for previous harsh remarks, a notable diplomatic signal as G7 leaders discuss pathways to ending the Russia-Ukraine war. (Kyiv Independent)

item.title: 🇺🇸✈️ US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards AFB, all 8 crew killed
item.description: A B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base in California, bursting into flames and killing all eight crew members aboard, including two Boeing employees. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇮🇷⚽ Iran soccer team blocked from training in US, welcomed by Tijuana amid World Cup
item.description: Iran's World Cup squad was denied entry to train in the United States amid the recent conflict, but the Mexican border city of Tijuana rolled out a welcome for the team caught between sport and geopolitics. (NPR)

item.title: 🇮🇱⚔️🇺🇸 Netanyahu and Trump on Collision Course as US-Iran Deal Falls Short of Israeli War Goals
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pushed back against the US-Iran peace agreement, declaring that "the struggle has not ended." The deal falls short of Israel's stated war objectives, creating a rift between the two allies at a critical moment. (Reuters)

item.title: ⛪🇺🇦💥 Historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Monastery Set Ablaze in Russian Strike
item.description: A nearly 1,000-year-old monastery — one of Ukraine's most sacred cultural sites with golden domes towering over Kyiv for centuries — was set ablaze during a Russian attack. Firefighters worked to contain the damage to the historic cathedral famed for its frescoes and silver artefacts. (Reuters)

item.title: ⚔️ Ukraine drives rush for mini drone laser-targeting systems as battlefield demand surges
item.description: Defense companies are racing to miniaturize laser target-marking technology for small UAVs after Ukraine requested compact systems suited to its drone-heavy battlefield tactics, spurring new product launches across the industry. (Defense News)

item.title: 🇨🇳 China develops military stealth coating that could be mass-produced as cheap house paint
item.description: Chinese researchers have created a new microwave-absorbing material that could make stealth technology far cheaper to produce, sparking debate over reports that commercial stealth paint may already be available on the open market. (SCMP)

item.title: 💰 Central banks accelerate gold buying as de-dollarization trend deepens worldwide
item.description: The World Gold Council reports that nearly 90% of central banks plan to increase gold holdings as countries diversify reserves away from the US dollar, reflecting a structural shift in global finance amid geopolitical fragmentation. (SCMP)

item.title: 🏛️ Congress reacts with skepticism to Trump-Iran deal, demands briefing on secret terms
item.description: Republican and Democratic senators alike expressed deep skepticism about the US-Iran agreement, with GOP hawks calling it a surrender and Democrats demanding immediate classified briefings on the deal’s undisclosed terms. (HuffPost)

item.title: 🇺🇸 JD Vance admits unresolved issues remain in Iran peace deal ahead of G7 talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged that several issues still remain in the US-Iran peace agreement as G7 leaders prepare to discuss the Middle East, tempering expectations for a finalized deal at the summit. (The Telegraph)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Iran deal offers exit from war but raises fresh political risks at home
item.description: The US-Iran peace agreement gives Trump a way out of the 3.5-month conflict, but skeptical conservatives in Congress are raising concerns over whether Iran will regain access to its frozen assets under the deal. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇨🇳🇪🇺 Surging Chinese exports threaten European economy, raising alarm at G7 summit
item.description: Eight years of US tariffs have failed to dent China industrial output, and a new wave of cheap exports is now pressuring European manufacturers, forcing G7 leaders to confront an escalating trade crisis. (AP News)

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 ECB chief Lagarde welcomes Iran ceasefire but warns inflation may persist
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde called the US-Iran ceasefire good news that could help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but several ECB colleagues cautioned it would not immediately bring down elevated euro zone inflation. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇳🇴⚖️ Norway Crown Princess son convicted of rape, sentenced to four years
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was found guilty of two counts of rape and sentenced to four years in prison in a case that has shaken the Norwegian royal family. (AP News)

item.title: 🇺🇦🔥 Ukrainian drone attack sets oil depot ablaze in Russia’s Krasnodar region near Crimea
item.description: A drone attack sparked a major fire at an oil depot in the village of Poltavskaya in Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, forcing the closure of a road connecting to annexed Crimea. The main fire was later extinguished. (Kyiv Independent)

item.title: 🇯🇵⛏️ Takaichi pushes mineral stockpiling plan at G7, seeks to bridge divides as Japan’s Iron Lady takes the stage
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi proposed a joint critical minerals stockpiling initiative at the G7 summit to counter China’s dominance, while also welcoming the US-Iran memorandum as a step toward regional de-escalation. (SCMP)
